old_claim.note: SmallSats & CubeSats provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct SmallSat or CubeSat dependency remains because coordinated Vela satellite pairs operated in 1963, decades before the standardized CubeSat platform introduced in 1999.

source_shape.source_locator: What are CubeSats? section: dates the standardized CubeSat platform to 1999, long after the scoped 1963 Vela constellation milestone.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: NASA's chronology places the CubeSat standard more than three decades after coordinated satellite constellations were already demonstrated.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Small spacecraft can make later constellations cheaper or denser, but a later form factor cannot be a prerequisite for an earlier system class built from conventional satellites.

ontology_before: The general constellation class was modeled as enabled by a much later small-satellite and CubeSat implementation family.
ontology_after: Constellations are scoped to coordinated multi-satellite operation independent of spacecraft size or CubeSat standardization.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: smallsat_cubesats is absent as a direct prerequisite of satellite_constellations.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed specifically to modern small-satellite mega-constellations rather than the general constellation architecture.
The CubeSat standard is authoritatively redated before the 1963 Vela pair.
short_reason: Satellite constellations existed decades before standardized CubeSats.
